article_title: Google Releases Gemma 4 Under Apache 2.0 License, Making Its Most Capable Open Models Fully Redistributable for the First Time

content_preview.title: Google Releases Gemma 4 Under Apache 2.0 License, Making Its Most Capable Open Models Fully Redistributable for the First Time
content_preview.summary: Google DeepMind shipped four Gemma 4 model variants on April 2 under the OSI-approved Apache 2.0 license, replacing the restrictive Gemma Terms of Use and removing all barriers to commercial deployment, modification, and redistribution.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 on April 2, introducing four model variants that range from lightweight edge deployments to a 31-billion-parameter dense model built on the same technology that powers the proprietary Gemini 3. The release marks a significant licensing shift: for the first time in the Gemma family's history, all models ship under the [OSI-approved Apache 2.0 license](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/gemma-4/), replacing the Gemma Term...

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive technical coverage of Gemma 4 with model specs, benchmarks, licensing context, and availability details. Well-structured.
editor_notes.source_verification: Google Blog: verified as official announcement. 9to5Google: verified — model lineup, specs, function-calling, AI Studio availability confirmed. The Decoder: verified — AIME 89.2% vs 20.8%, GPQA Diamond 85.7%, 26B MoE 3.8B active params, H100 GPU, hardware compatibility all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All benchmark numbers, model specs, and licensing claims verified against sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Technical without being inaccessible.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior Gemma 4 coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality AI/open-source article with strong technical detail and proper attribution. Approved.
